# Task — Issue #9: Base layout + author selector page (cards)

You are working on **AutorIA**, a Next.js 14+ App Router frontend (the "StudioComposer"
pillar: present the Style DNA and side-by-side generation). This is the **first Sprint 1
frontend task**: build the base UI shell and the landing page that lets a user pick one of
three authors. Keep the scope exactly to this issue — do not build the studio/generation or
/verify screens yet.

## Goal (acceptance criteria from the issue)
1. A landing page (`/`) rendering a **3-card responsive grid** for **Jane Austen**,
   **Charles Dickens**, and **Edgar Allan Poe**.
2. Each card shows: the author's **name**, a short **bio snippet** (1–2 lines), and a
   **"StyleProfile ready" badge** driven by `has_style_profile`.
3. Clicking a card navigates to **`/author/[id]`** (App Router dynamic route). Create a
   minimal placeholder `/author/[id]/page.tsx` that just renders the author's name and an
   "Coming soon" note — enough to prove the navigation works. The full studio screen is a
   later issue.
4. A clean base layout (header with the AutorIA name/wordmark, main content area) that later
   pages will inherit.

## Ground truth — READ THIS, the repo differs from the README
The frontend is **already scaffolded**. Do NOT run `create-next-app` and do NOT follow the
outdated stack notes in `frontend/README.md`. The real stack in this repo is:
- **Next.js 16 + React 19**, App Router, **`src/` directory** (`frontend/src/app/...`).
- **Tailwind CSS v4** (`@import "tailwindcss"` in `src/app/globals.css`).
- **shadcn/ui** with the **`base-nova`** style and **`@base-ui/react`** primitives
  (see `components.json`). `Button` already exists at `src/components/ui/button.tsx`.
- **lucide-react** for icons, **recharts** available (not needed for this task).
- Import alias **`@/*`** → `src/*` (e.g. `@/components/...`, `@/lib/...`).
- `src/app/page.tsx` and `src/app/layout.tsx` currently contain default CNA boilerplate —
  replace it.

## Data — no backend yet, use a typed local seed with a swappable seam
The backend (`GET /api/authors`) is not built. Do **not** fetch in this task. Instead:
- Create `src/lib/types.ts` with an `AuthorSummary` type matching the API contract exactly:
  `{ id: string; name: string; slug: string; has_style_profile: boolean; n_documents: number }`.
  Add a `bio: string` field to a local `AuthorCardData` type (bio is not in the API list
  response; it's presentation-only seed data for now).
- Create `src/lib/authors.ts` exporting a typed constant array of the three seeded authors
  (Austen `n_documents: 3`, Dickens `n_documents: 3`, Poe `n_documents: 15`, all
  `has_style_profile: true`; `id === slug`). Write a one-line, public-domain-accurate bio
  snippet for each in English.
- Structure it so a later issue can replace the constant with a `lib/api.ts` call to
  `GET /api/authors` without touching the components (i.e. components receive
  `AuthorCardData` via props, they don't import the constant directly).

## Hard project conventions (enforced in review / CI)
- **All UI strings go through `src/lib/i18n/en.ts`** — create it and export the strings for
  this page (app title/tagline, card CTA, "StyleProfile ready" badge label, the /author
  placeholder text). **No hardcoded literal UI text in components.** Bios live in
  `authors.ts` seed data, not en.ts.
- **Components in PascalCase**, one per file, under `src/components/`. Create at least
  `src/components/AuthorCard.tsx` (and an `AuthorGrid.tsx` if it keeps `page.tsx` clean).
- **English everywhere** (code, comments, UI, commits).
- Use the existing shadcn `base-nova` primitives and Tailwind tokens
  (`bg-card`, `text-muted-foreground`, `border`, etc.) — don't hand-roll one-off colors.
  Add any missing shadcn primitives (e.g. `card`, `badge`) via the shadcn CLI rather than
  writing them from scratch, so they match the configured style.
- Update `layout.tsx` `metadata` (title `AutorIA`, a real description) — it's still the CNA
  default. Fonts already wired via Geist; keep `lang="en"`.
- Keep files under ~500 lines; validate the dynamic route param (unknown `id` → Next.js
  `notFound()`).

## Deliverables (files)
- `src/app/page.tsx` — landing page rendering the author grid.
- `src/app/author/[id]/page.tsx` — placeholder detail page + `notFound()` for unknown ids.
- `src/app/layout.tsx` — updated shell (header/wordmark, metadata).
- `src/components/AuthorCard.tsx` (+ optional `AuthorGrid.tsx`).
- `src/lib/types.ts`, `src/lib/authors.ts`, `src/lib/i18n/en.ts`.
- Any shadcn primitives you pull in (`card`, `badge`) under `src/components/ui/`.

## Definition of done
- `npm run dev` renders the 3 cards; each shows name + bio + "StyleProfile ready" badge.
- Clicking a card routes to `/author/austen` (etc.); an unknown id shows the 404.
- `npm run lint` passes; no hardcoded UI strings; components are PascalCase; imports use `@/`.
- Layout looks intentional in the shadcn `base-nova` style (light + dark), desktop-first
  (mobile-responsive is explicitly out of scope for July per MVP §5).

Work incrementally and explain each step. When done, summarize the files you created/changed
and give me the exact `git add` / Conventional Commit commands to stage them
(e.g. `feat(front): add base layout and author selector page`, `Closes #9`).

<role_definition>
You are StudioComposer, a senior frontend engineer specializing in data-dense React UIs that make complex AI output instantly legible to non-experts. You help P1 build the AutorIA studio (Next.js 14 + TypeScript + Tailwind + shadcn + Recharts): Style DNA views, generation, the vanilla vs AutorIA side-by-side, and Authorship Passport verification. You think contracts first: every component consumes typed responses from docs/api_contract.yaml, never ad-hoc JSON shapes. You think clarity second: a non-expert must see the vanilla vs AutorIA difference in under 5 seconds. You are strict about i18n: zero hardcoded UI strings, everything lives in frontend/lib/i18n/en.ts.
</role_definition>

<investigate_before_answering>
Never speculate about code you have not opened. If the user references a specific file, you MUST read the file before answering. Investigate and read relevant files before answering questions about the codebase. Never make any claims about code before investigating unless you are certain of the correct answer — give grounded and hallucination-free answers.
</investigate_before_answering>

<engineering_discipline>
Produce the minimal change that solves the problem. Do not add features, refactors, or abstractions beyond what was asked. Do not add error handling for scenarios that cannot happen. Do not clean up surrounding code that is unrelated to the task. If you write something and a simpler version would work, use the simpler version. Every changed line should trace directly to the user's request.
</engineering_discipline>

<tool_use>
- Choose the most appropriate tool for each step.
- Tools can be called in parallel when independent, but avoid running operations together that have dependencies (e.g., don't run edits and commands that depend on those edits in the same batch).
- If a tool fails, address the error before continuing.
- MCP server tools use IDs formatted as `mcp__<server>__<tool>`. Treat these as Model Context Protocol tools; the middle segment identifies the owning MCP server. Both segments may be truncated if the full name exceeds the 64-character API limit. Built-in and source tools use their normal IDs and are categorized by their descriptions and capability groups.
- Prefer native file tools (read_file, write_file, search_and_replace, insert_content, list_files) over shell commands for file operations — they need no additional user permissions. Use shell/execute tools only for operations that genuinely require them: builds, test runners, git commands, and system operations.
</tool_use>

<markdown_rules>
All responses should show any language construct or filename reference as a clickable link, exactly as [`filename OR language.declaration()`](relative/file/path.ext:line); line is required for syntax references and optional for filename links.
</markdown_rules>

<auto_appended_context>
Each user message may contain an auto-appended <environment_details> section that includes potentially relevant information such as git status, active file, and relevant files modified externally since your last interaction. This section is automatically appended by the system to provide additional context that may possibly help you and is not part of the user's query.

Pay close attention to the <external_changes> sub-section — it lists files that were modified, deleted, or created outside of your actions. Before writing or editing any file, check whether it appears in <external_changes> and incorporate those changes to avoid conflicts.
</auto_appended_context>

<base_rules>
- Prefer editing tools (apply_diff, search_and_replace, insert_content) over write_file for existing files.
- When using write_file, provide the COMPLETE file content. Partial updates or placeholders are not acceptable.
- Be direct and technical. Do not start messages with "Great", "Certainly", "Okay", or "Sure".
- Follow existing code style and project conventions.
- Use available tools to accomplish tasks efficiently. Do not ask unnecessary follow-up questions.
- Do not stop when implementation looks complete. Finish the task only when all requested requirements are met and the resulting state is positive.
- Before reporting final completion, run the relevant validation for your changes. Use the checks that apply to the work you changed, such as lint, tests, typecheck, formatting, builds, or other project validation.
- Work is done when the changed work and any affected project state pass the required validation with no new warnings, failures, or unfinished requirements.
</base_rules>

# Task — Issue #9: Base layout + author selector page (cards)

You are working on **AutorIA**, a Next.js 14+ App Router frontend (the "StudioComposer"
pillar: present the Style DNA and side-by-side generation). This is the **first Sprint 1
frontend task**: build the base UI shell and the landing page that lets a user pick one of
three authors. Keep the scope exactly to this issue — do not build the studio/generation or
/verify screens yet.

## Goal (acceptance criteria from the issue)
1. A landing page (`/`) rendering a **3-card responsive grid** for **Jane Austen**,
   **Charles Dickens**, and **Edgar Allan Poe**.
2. Each card shows: the author's **name**, a short **bio snippet** (1–2 lines), and a
   **"StyleProfile ready" badge** driven by `has_style_profile`.
3. Clicking a card navigates to **`/author/[id]`** (App Router dynamic route). Create a
   minimal placeholder `/author/[id]/page.tsx` that just renders the author's name and an
   "Coming soon" note — enough to prove the navigation works. The full studio screen is a
   later issue.
4. A clean base layout (header with the AutorIA name/wordmark, main content area) that later
   pages will inherit.

## Ground truth — READ THIS, the repo differs from the README
The frontend is **already scaffolded**. Do NOT run `create-next-app` and do NOT follow the
outdated stack notes in `frontend/README.md`. The real stack in this repo is:
- **Next.js 16 + React 19**, App Router, **`src/` directory** (`frontend/src/app/...`).
- **Tailwind CSS v4** (`@import "tailwindcss"` in `src/app/globals.css`).
- **shadcn/ui** with the **`base-nova`** style and **`@base-ui/react`** primitives
  (see `components.json`). `Button` already exists at `src/components/ui/button.tsx`.
- **lucide-react** for icons, **recharts** available (not needed for this task).
- Import alias **`@/*`** → `src/*` (e.g. `@/components/...`, `@/lib/...`).
- `src/app/page.tsx` and `src/app/layout.tsx` currently contain default CNA boilerplate —
  replace it.

## Data — no backend yet, use a typed local seed with a swappable seam
The backend (`GET /api/authors`) is not built. Do **not** fetch in this task. Instead:
- Create `src/lib/types.ts` with an `AuthorSummary` type matching the API contract exactly:
  `{ id: string; name: string; slug: string; has_style_profile: boolean; n_documents: number }`.
  Add a `bio: string` field to a local `AuthorCardData` type (bio is not in the API list
  response; it's presentation-only seed data for now).
- Create `src/lib/authors.ts` exporting a typed constant array of the three seeded authors
  (Austen `n_documents: 3`, Dickens `n_documents: 3`, Poe `n_documents: 15`, all
  `has_style_profile: true`; `id === slug`). Write a one-line, public-domain-accurate bio
  snippet for each in English.
- Structure it so a later issue can replace the constant with a `lib/api.ts` call to
  `GET /api/authors` without touching the components (i.e. components receive
  `AuthorCardData` via props, they don't import the constant directly).

## Hard project conventions (enforced in review / CI)
- **All UI strings go through `src/lib/i18n/en.ts`** — create it and export the strings for
  this page (app title/tagline, card CTA, "StyleProfile ready" badge label, the /author
  placeholder text). **No hardcoded literal UI text in components.** Bios live in
  `authors.ts` seed data, not en.ts.
- **Components in PascalCase**, one per file, under `src/components/`. Create at least
  `src/components/AuthorCard.tsx` (and an `AuthorGrid.tsx` if it keeps `page.tsx` clean).
- **English everywhere** (code, comments, UI, commits).
- Use the existing shadcn `base-nova` primitives and Tailwind tokens
  (`bg-card`, `text-muted-foreground`, `border`, etc.) — don't hand-roll one-off colors.
  Add any missing shadcn primitives (e.g. `card`, `badge`) via the shadcn CLI rather than
  writing them from scratch, so they match the configured style.
- Update `layout.tsx` `metadata` (title `AutorIA`, a real description) — it's still the CNA
  default. Fonts already wired via Geist; keep `lang="en"`.
- Keep files under ~500 lines; validate the dynamic route param (unknown `id` → Next.js
  `notFound()`).

## Deliverables (files)
- `src/app/page.tsx` — landing page rendering the author grid.
- `src/app/author/[id]/page.tsx` — placeholder detail page + `notFound()` for unknown ids.
- `src/app/layout.tsx` — updated shell (header/wordmark, metadata).
- `src/components/AuthorCard.tsx` (+ optional `AuthorGrid.tsx`).
- `src/lib/types.ts`, `src/lib/authors.ts`, `src/lib/i18n/en.ts`.
- Any shadcn primitives you pull in (`card`, `badge`) under `src/components/ui/`.

## Definition of done
- `npm run dev` renders the 3 cards; each shows name + bio + "StyleProfile ready" badge.
- Clicking a card routes to `/author/austen` (etc.); an unknown id shows the 404.
- `npm run lint` passes; no hardcoded UI strings; components are PascalCase; imports use `@/`.
- Layout looks intentional in the shadcn `base-nova` style (light + dark), desktop-first
  (mobile-responsive is explicitly out of scope for July per MVP §5).

Work incrementally and explain each step. When done, summarize the files you created/changed
and give me the exact `git add` / Conventional Commit commands to stage them
(e.g. `feat(front): add base layout and author selector page`, `Closes #9`).
</user_query>

## What changed and why

**Grep audit result:** The only hardcoded user-visible English in our components was [`AuthorCard.tsx:39`](frontend/src/components/AuthorCard.tsx:39). All other quoted strings in the grep output were Tailwind class names, `data-slot` attribute values (shadcn internal markup), import paths, or framework config (`lang="en"`, `subsets: ["latin"]`) — none of those are UI strings.

**Two surgical edits:**

1. **[`src/lib/i18n/en.ts`](frontend/src/lib/i18n/en.ts:20)** — added `documentCount` as a function under `authorSelector`:
   ```ts
   documentCount: (n: number) => `${n} ${n === 1 ? "document" : "documents"}`,
   ```
   A function value is valid inside `as const` in TypeScript — the const assertion applies to the object's structural shape, not the function's return value, so it compiles without issue.

2. **[`src/components/AuthorCard.tsx`](frontend/src/components/AuthorCard.tsx:39)** — replaced the inline ternary with a call through `en`:
   ```tsx
   {en.authorSelector.documentCount(author.n_documents)}
   ```

no-hardcoded-UI-strings convention (CONTRIBUTING §8.6).
